_comment: K14 write-scope violation fixture corpus (PR-4a). <<ROOT>> is substituted with the test's hermetic tmp worktree root at load time; <<OUTSIDE>> with a sibling dir OUTSIDE the root. No real-shaped secrets (F22/F23). Per ADR-0011 §write-scope-violations-schema the violation element shape is {path, kind, transport, detected_at_phase, sha256_pre, sha256_post, flags}. In v3.0-alpha the ONLY transport is 'snapshot' (ADR-0010 Decision 2 — pure snapshot, no event-stream); the three logical transports below (content-hash, mtime+hash, large-file-hash) are SNAPSHOT sub-strategies the snapshot leaf dispatches over file size — they all set element.transport='snapshot' so the resolver sees one transport. The 4 violation classes × 3 snapshot sub-strategies = 12 expected-violation fixtures the snapshot path must produce.
transport_note: v3.0-alpha element.transport is ALWAYS 'snapshot'. The 'snapshot_strategy' field below selects the within-snapshot hashing path (small-file content-hash | mtime+content-hash | large-file hash-only) so every fixture exercises the snapshot leaf, never an event-stream stub.

fixtures.note: small file written OUTSIDE the worktree root — content-hash snapshot strategy detects the change; checkWithinRoot rejects the path as absolute-outside-root
mtime+content-hash strategy — both mtime advanced AND content changed; still one snapshot transport
large file (>1MB) — hash-only snapshot strategy (no mtime fast-path), content-hash differs by one byte

CRITICAL security fixture: a symlink INSIDE the worktree resolving OUTSIDE the root is flagged symlink-escape and NOT hashed as in-scope (sha_pre/post stay null — the target bytes are never read into the in-scope hash)
symlinked ANCESTOR directory escapes root — the file under it must be flagged symlink-escape via checkWithinRoot (escapes-root), never hashed
symlink to a real system dir (/etc, neutral non-secret) — flagged, never followed into a hash; fail-closed on the symlink-escape resolution
F1 anchor: write lands 1000ms AFTER spawn_close_wall_ms but WITHIN the 5000ms tail-window → still attributed (detected_at_phase=tail-window). Uses an INJECTABLE clock, never wallclock.
edge: write at 4999ms (just inside the 5000ms tail-window boundary) is still attributed — injectable clock asserts the boundary precisely

NEGATIVE tail-window fixture: a write 6000ms after close — PAST the 5000ms tail-window — is NOT attributed to this spawn (expect_violation=false). The injectable clock proves the boundary is the gate, not the wallclock.

F7 (blair-HIGH-3): a file changed by a parent-environment process (IDE formatter / file watcher) that is NOT reachable from the spawn worktree root is flagged parent-scope-suspected AND carries the K14_SUSPECTED_FALSE_POSITIVE flag so the resolver can down-weight it
JetBrains IDE workspace churn — parent-environment write, mtime+hash strategy, suspected-false-positive flag set
a large cache file rewritten in place by a parent-environment build watcher — same size, different content; hash-only strategy still detects, suspected-false-positive flag set
F19 default-empty advisory: an in-scope file with IDENTICAL pre/post content produces ZERO violations → write_scope_violations defaults to []. The clean case is the contract's identity element.
an IN-SCOPE write (content changed but path resolves WITHIN root) is NOT a violation — write-scope enforcement only flags out-of-scope / symlink-escape / parent-suspected
